source_target: Austin Firefighters Association Local 975 collective bargaining agreement
resolved_url_or_lookup_path: https://www.austintexas.gov/labor-relations/fire-collective-bargaining-agreement -> official page labels this the 'Current Agreement': https://austin.widen.net/view/pdf/berk4a8tuc/APPENDIX-A.pdf?t.download=true
reason: Official austintexas.gov labor-relations page verbatim-labels this document 'Collective Bargaining Agreement between the City of Austin and Austin Firefighters Association Local 975 -- Effective September 8, 2023 - September 30, 2025' as the Current Agreement, distinct from ~24 negotiation-process documents (redlines, proposals, ground rules) for a still-in-flight Dec.2025 successor listed further down the same page. Downloaded and text-extracted via ingest/extract_text.py: 100 pages, clean text layer, full numbered articles (Preamble, Definitions, Recognition of Association, Management Rights, Wages & Benefits, etc.), Article 30 Section 1 confirms term runs through September 30, 2025 and ties the effective date to an interest-arbitration award.
caveat: A Dec. 18, 2025 successor agreement was ratified by City Council per news reporting (KUT, Community Impact) but the officially posted 'current agreement' label and the single clean full-text document remain the 2023-2025 CBA; the successor exists only as a set of separate negotiation-session/redline documents on the official page, not one clean executed copy, so it was not selected as the fetch target this session.
notes: This resolves the exact held-out target from texas_ohio_acquisition_dry_run_2026-07-08.csv row 6 (needs_url_confirmation -> resolved). Same austin.widen.net hosting family already used for the ingested tx_austin_police_2024 row.

source_target: AFSCME Local 1624 consultation agreement (City Council Resolution No. 20260122-049, adopted 2026-02-26)
reason: The specific document is now located and identified (a Council-adopted consultation-policy resolution establishing regular meetings between AFSCME Local 1624 and the City Manager's Office over employment policy, working conditions, reorganization, and AI/automation use), resolving the design question a prior session flagged as open. It is a consultation/voice mechanism, not a negotiated collective bargaining agreement: no exclusive-bargaining-agent recognition clause, no wage schedule, no negotiated compensation terms. It does not meet this project's causal-corpus definition ('text that causes wages') or the CBA source-verification standard's 'wage-setting' bar.
caveat: Do not ingest as a contracts.csv row under any occupation_class; Austin non-safety wages continue to be set unilaterally via the civil-service classification/pay-plan system (see next row), not through this consultation channel.
notes: This closes the open design question from texas_ohio_approved_source_plan_2026-07-08.csv row 22 (backup) -- the consultation agreement does exist and is confirmed, but is confirmed NOT to qualify as a matched non-safety CBA. Austin therefore has no matched non-safety comparison unit as of this run; flagged in the summary as an open coverage gap, not silently dropped.
